transcript: Respondents cast a total of 8 votes. 6 of them voted for the third step of the ladder. This indicates that females of this village have power & freedom to make some major life decisions.
Most of us can take a few decisions
Yes, our parents listen to us
We are taught to speak up for ourselves and we do
When our parents decide to send us to school they decided to empower us
They send us to school because times are changing and that is the need of the hour
And so they listen to us too
Education has brought about a revolutionary change ľ we are wiser and more capable
We can distinguish between good and bad
It is essential that we study otherwise we will not progress in life
If we donĺt go to school we will never learn all the new things that we do; if we donĺt study we will only be rolling chapatis and bearing children
Education opens our eyes to the world
Education is necessary for our societyĺs and nationĺs progress
We will get respect only if we are educated
Boys study as much as they want
They usually complete their graduation

We need to be a graduate to get a good job
It is good to study as much as we can
Graduation is a minimum requirement for any job
No

No, times have changed and now parents are equally conscious about educating both

Now parents are not partial to boys; that was the case many years ago; now they want both boys and girls to study

Poverty, Early marriage, Security concerns.

some quit because of transport concerns to college.

The two most important reasons on this list why girls end their education.
Poverty
Early marriage
In many cases students have to drop out because there is no money to pay for their school/college
No, I did not have any specific goal in mind
I wanted to do tailoring but could not
We have plans but they donĺt always materialize
Who doesnĺt have dreams and goals; I wanted to be a teacher; I am a graduate and will be getting married soon; I donĺt know if I will be allowed to work at my husbandĺs place
I am also a graduate and I wanted to work but there is no opportunity in our village; now my parents are planning to get me married
Young men do agriculture

The lucky ones have jobs

áZero tiller, Harvester, Combine, Cultivator, Rotavator, Pump, set Highmatic spray (for pesticides)á

combine

Yes, they supervise the work

Men also like machines because it makes their work easier and they get that much extra time
Women donĺt use combine because it is a very heavy machine

Important wheat innovation for women Now
Agriculture has become mechanized. We now have zero tiller, rotavator, cultivator, combine which have all made human work so much easier
Important wheat innovation for women 10 years ago
Then we had a tractor which became very popular. Then some people had motor pumps and spray machines too
Now there is more awareness
Everyone does agriculture in villages and they do it the way they saw their forefathers doing it; but now some people go outside and learn new things which they bring back to the village
Organizations like CSISA help spread awareness about new technologies, seeds and machines
Lot of new things are also taught at school which we discuss with our parents
Traditionally men and women have performed different roles in agriculture but that is gradually changing

Helping their fathers is one thing and doing it all alone a completely different game

It will depend upon the mentality of the people ľ some may appreciate while others will chastise her

He will be happy that his wife is doing well
